---
title: "contradiction_attack_stat_modelFitting"
author: "Henrike Beyer"
date: "2025-11-11"
output: html_document
---


```{r}
library(lme4)
library(broom.mixed)
library(dplyr)
```


This is the notebook documenting the data inspection and analysis for the ARG-NLI project. It will document the inspection of distributions of NLI and ARG labels, as well as their overlap. It documents also the distribution of categorical and continuous predictors by label (box / violin plots). 

```{r}
hard_threshold <- read.csv("D:\\OneDrive - University of Dundee\\PhD\\ARG-NLI_project\\feature_analysis\\automatic\\new_features\\corpus_threshold_hard_0.999_standardised_and_log_scaled.csv")

factor_vars <- c(
  "seg1_has_deduction", "seg2_has_deduction",
  "seg1_has_refutation", "seg2_has_refutation", 
  "seg1_has_condition", "seg2_has_condition",
  "seg1_has_explanation", "seg2_has_explanation", 
  "seg1_has_adverb_of_emphasis", "seg2_has_adverb_of_emphasis",
  "seg1_has_justification", "seg2_has_justification", 
  "seg1_has_modal_verbs", "seg2_has_modal_verbs",
  "seg1_senti", "seg2_senti"
)

hard_threshold[factor_vars] <- lapply(hard_threshold[factor_vars], function(x) as.factor(as.character(x)))

```

```{r}
blocks <- list(
  lexical_markers = c("seg1_has_deduction", "seg2_has_deduction",
                      "seg1_has_refutation", "seg2_has_refutation", 
                      "seg1_has_condition", "seg2_has_condition",
                      "seg1_has_explanation", "seg2_has_explanation", 
                      "seg1_has_adverb_of_emphasis", "seg2_has_adverb_of_emphasis",
                      "seg1_has_justification", "seg2_has_justification", 
                      "seg1_has_modal_verbs", "seg2_has_modal_verbs"),
  lexical_sim = c("tfidf_cosine_similarity"),
  semantic = c("cosine_similarity"),
  structure = c("seg1_sentence_length_mean", "seg2_sentence_length_mean"),
  #                     "seg1_n_tokens", "seg2_n_tokens"),
  syntactic = c("seg1_dependency_distance_mean", "seg2_dependency_distance_mean",
                "seg1_prop_adjacent_dependency_relation_mean", "seg2_prop_adjacent_dependency_relation_mean",
                "seg1_avg_subclauses", "seg2_avg_subclauses",
                "seg1_avg_tree_depth", "seg2_avg_tree_depth"),
  perplexity = c("seg1_perplexity", "seg2_perplexity", #"seg1_given_seg2_perplexity", 
                 "seg2_given_seg1_perplexity"),
  indirectness = c("seg1_impScore", "seg2_impScore", "prag_distance"),
  sentiment = c("seg1_senti", "seg2_senti")
)

interactions <- list(
  c("seg1_avg_subclauses", "seg1_sentence_length_mean"),
  c("seg2_avg_subclauses", "seg2_sentence_length_mean"),
  c("seg1_avg_tree_depth", "seg1_avg_subclauses"),
  c("seg2_avg_tree_depth", "seg2_avg_subclauses"),
  c("seg1_avg_tree_depth", "seg1_sentence_length_mean"),
  c("seg2_avg_tree_depth", "seg2_sentence_length_mean"),
  c("seg1_dependency_distance_mean", "seg1_sentence_length_mean"),
  c("seg2_sentence_length_mean", "seg2_dependency_distance_mean"),
  c("seg1_dependency_distance_mean", "seg1_avg_subclauses"),
  c("prag_distance", "cosine_similarity"),
  c("tfidf_cosine_similarity", "cosine_similarity"),
  c("seg2_dependency_distance_mean", "seg2_avg_subclauses"),
  c("seg2_sentence_length_mean", "seg2_n_tokens"),
  c("seg2_avg_subclauses", "seg2_n_tokens"),
  c("seg1_avg_subclauses", "seg1_n_tokens"),
  c("seg1_sentence_length_mean", "seg1_n_tokens"),
  c("seg2_dependency_distance_mean", "seg2_n_tokens"),
  c("tfidf_cosine_similarity", "prag_distance"),
  c("seg1_avg_tree_depth", "seg1_dependency_distance_mean"),
  c("seg2_avg_tree_depth", "seg2_n_tokens"),
  c("seg1_dependency_distance_mean", "seg1_n_tokens"),
  c("seg1_avg_tree_depth", "seg1_n_tokens"),
  c("seg2_dependency_distance_mean", "seg2_prop_adjacent_dependency_relation_mean"),
  c("seg1_prop_adjacent_dependency_relation_mean", "seg1_dependency_distance_mean")
)

```

```{r}
# Combine all variables you plan to use anywhere
all_predictors <- unique(unlist(c(blocks, lapply(interactions, unlist))))
all_vars <- c(all_predictors, "CorpusID", "arg_nli_attack", "arg_nli_support")

# Drop rows with any missing values in those columns
hard_threshold_complete <- hard_threshold[complete.cases(hard_threshold[all_vars]), ]

attack_only_df <- hard_threshold_complete %>% filter(is_attack == 1)
attack_only_df$CorpusID <- as.factor(attack_only_df$CorpusID)

# From your support-only data,
# select only the columns needed for the model,
# and then drop any row that has an NA in ANY of those columns.
attack_clean_df <- attack_only_df %>%
  select(all_of(all_vars)) %>%
  na.omit()

# Check the before and after (optional, but good)
message("Rows in support_only_df: ", nrow(attack_only_df))
message("Rows in attack_clean_df (after removing NAs): ", nrow(attack_clean_df))
```


```{r}
vars_to_formula <- function(vars, interactions = NULL) {
  f <- paste(vars, collapse = " + ")
  if (!is.null(interactions) && length (interactions) > 0) {
    int_terms <- sapply(interactions, function(x) paste(x, collapse = ":"))
  }
  f
}
```


```{r}
is_contradiction <- "is_entailment"
is_contradiction <- "is_contradiction"
is_neutral <- "is_neutral"
is_support <- "is_support"
is_attack <- "is_attack"
support_is_entailment <- "arg_nli_support"
attack_is_contradiction <- "arg_nli_attack"

```

```{r}
fit_blockwise_models <- function(df, outcome, blocks, interactions=NULL,
                                 random = "(1 | CorpusID)", family = binomial,
                                 base_controls = c("seg1_n_tokens", "seg2_n_tokens"),
                                 add_interactions_last = TRUE) {
  models <- list()
  results <- tibble()
  
  # baseline model
  base_formula <- paste0(outcome, " ~ ", paste(base_controls, collapse = " + "), " + ", random)
  message("Fitting baseline model: ", base_formula)
  m_base <- glmer(as.formula(base_formula), data = df,
                  family = family, control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))
  models[["baseline"]] <- m_base
  results <- bind_rows(results, tibble(block = "baseline", AIC = AIC(m_base)))
  
  # accumulate variables and add blocks
  current_vars <- base_controls
  block_names <- names(blocks)
  
  for (b in block_names) {
    current_vars <- c(current_vars, blocks[[b]])
    fstring <- paste0(outcome, " ~ ", vars_to_formula(current_vars), " + ", random)
    message("Fitting block: ", b)
    m <- glmer(as.formula(fstring), data = df, family = family,
               control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))
    models[[b]] <- m
    
    comp <- anova(models[[length(models)-1]], m, test = "Chisq")
    results <- bind_rows(results,
      tibble(block = b,
             AIC = AIC(m),
             delta_AIC = diff(comp$AIC),
             LRT = comp$Chisq[2],
             p = comp$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2]))
  }
  
  # optional final model with all interactions
  if (add_interactions_last && !is.null(interactions)) {
    fstring_full <- paste0(outcome, " ~ ",
                           vars_to_formula(unlist(blocks, use.names = FALSE), interactions),
                           " + ", random)
    message("Fitting FULL model (all blocks + interactions)")
    m_full <- glmer(as.formula(fstring_full), data = df,
                    family = family, control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))
    models[["full"]] <- m_full
    results <- bind_rows(results,
      tibble(block = "full",
             AIC = AIC(m_full),
             delta_AIC = NA,
             LRT = NA,
             p = NA))
  }
  
  list(models = models, comparison = results)
}
```

```{r}
fit_block_models_safe <- function(df, outcome, blocks, random = "(1 | CorpusID)",
                                  base_controls = c("seg1_n_tokens", "seg2_n_tokens"),
                                  family = binomial) {
  library(lme4)
  library(performance)
  library(dplyr)
  
  models <- list()
  results <- tibble()
  
  # baseline model
  base_formula <- paste0(outcome, " ~ ", paste(base_controls, collapse = " + "), " + ", random)
  message("Fitting baseline model: ", base_formula)
  m_base <- glmer(as.formula(base_formula), data = df,
                  family = family, control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))
  models[["baseline"]] <- m_base
  results <- bind_rows(results, tibble(
    block = "baseline", AIC = AIC(m_base),
    delta_AIC = NA, LRT = NA, p = NA, r2_marg = NA
  ))
  
  # loop over blocks
  for (b in names(blocks)) {
    vars <- c(base_controls, blocks[[b]])
    fstring <- paste0(outcome, " ~ ", paste(vars, collapse = " + "), " + ", random)
    message("Fitting block-only model: ", b)
    
    m <- tryCatch(
      glmer(as.formula(fstring), data = df, family = family,
            control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa")),
      error = function(e) { warning("Model failed for block ", b, ": ", e$message); return(NULL) }
    )
    
    if (!is.null(m)) {
      models[[b]] <- m
      comp <- tryCatch(anova(m_base, m, test = "Chisq"), error = function(e) NULL)
      r2 <- tryCatch(performance::r2_nakagawa(m)$R2_marginal, error = function(e) NA)
      
      row <- tibble(
        block = b,
        AIC = AIC(m),
        delta_AIC = if (!is.null(comp)) AIC(m_base) - AIC(m) else NA,
        LRT = if (!is.null(comp)) comp$Chisq[2] else NA,
        p = if (!is.null(comp)) comp$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2] else NA,
        r2_marg = r2
      )
      results <- bind_rows(results, row)
    } else {
      results <- bind_rows(results, tibble(
        block = b, AIC = NA, delta_AIC = NA, LRT = NA, p = NA, r2_marg = NA
      ))
    }
  }
  
  list(models = models, summary = results)
}
```


```{r}
outcome <- attack_is_contradiction

block_results <- fit_block_models_safe(attack_only_df, outcome = outcome, blocks = blocks)

block_results$summary %>%
  arrange(AIC)  # smaller AIC = better fit
```
```{r}
block_results$summary %>%
  arrange(AIC)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(block_results$summary[-1,], aes(x = reorder(block, -delta_AIC), y = delta_AIC)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Feature Block", y = "ΔAIC improvement over baseline",
       title = "Relative contribution of feature blocks")
```
```{r}
extract_block_details <- function(block_results) {
  library(broom.mixed)
  lapply(names(block_results$models), function(b) {
    m <- block_results$models[[b]]
    if (is.null(m)) return(NULL)
    broom.mixed::tidy(m, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
      mutate(block = b)
  }) %>% bind_rows() %>% filter(term != "(Intercept)")
}
```

```{r}
block_details <- extract_block_details(block_results)
```

```{r}
library(car)
car::Anova(block_results$models[["semantic"]])
car::Anova(block_results$models[["indirectness"]])
car::Anova(block_results$models[["perplexity"]])
car::Anova(block_results$models[["lexical_sim"]])
```


```{r}
strong_blocks <- c("semantic", "indirectness", "perplexity", "lexical_sim")

sig_features <- block_details %>%
  filter(block %in% strong_blocks,
         p.value < 0.05,
         abs(estimate - 1) > 0.05) %>%
  arrange(block, p.value) %>%
  select(block, term, estimate, p.value, conf.low, conf.high)

sig_features
```

```{r}
unique(sig_features$term)
```


```{r}
final_features <- c("prag_distance",
                    "seg2_n_tokens",
                    "seg2_impScore",
                    "seg1_impScore",
                    "seg1_n_tokens",
                    "tfidf_cosine_similarity",
                    "seg2_given_seg1_perplexity",
                    "seg2_perplexity",
                    "seg1_perplexity",
                    "cosine_similarity"
)

f_final <- as.formula(
  paste("arg_nli_attack ~", paste(final_features, collapse = " + "), "+ (1 | CorpusID)")
)

model_att_con_baseline <-glmer(arg_nli_attack ~ (1|CorpusID) + seg1_n_tokens + seg2_n_tokens, data = attack_clean_df,
                       family = binomial,
                       control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))

model_att_con_blockFeatures <- glmer(f_final, data = attack_clean_df,
                     family = binomial,
                     control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))
```

```{r}
#model_with_length <- glmer(arg_nli_attack ~ (1|CorpusID) 
#                        + cosine_similarity 
#                        + prag_distance
#                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity 
#                        + seg1_perplexity 
#                        + seg2_perplexity
#                        + seg2_n_tokens
#                        + seg1_n_tokens
#                        + seg1_impScore
#                        + seg2_impScore
#                        + tfidf_cosine_similarity, 
#                        data = hard_threshold_complete, family = binomial, control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))
```

```{r}
model_att_con_without_length <- glmer(arg_nli_attack ~ (1|CorpusID) 
                        + prag_distance
                        #+ seg2_n_tokens
                        + seg2_impScore
                        + seg1_impScore
                        #+ seg1_n_tokens
                        + tfidf_cosine_similarity
                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity
                        + seg2_perplexity
                        + seg1_perplexity
                        + cosine_similarity
                        , data = attack_clean_df, family = binomial, control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))
```

```{r}
library(car)

anova(model_att_con_baseline, model_att_con_blockFeatures, model_att_con_without_length, test = "Chisq")
message("R2 model block features")
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_att_con_blockFeatures)
#message("R2 with length")
#performance::r2_nakagawa(model_with_length)
message("R2 without lenght")
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_att_con_without_length)
vif(model_att_con_without_length)
#vif(model_with_length)
vif(model_supp_ent_without_length)
#vif(model_interact_with_length)
```

```{r}
# --- 1. Load the necessary libraries ---
library(tidyverse)
library(fastDummies) # For handling multi-level factors

# --- 2. Get predictor names ---
# Use the formula for your 22-predictor 'model_final' for 'arg_nli_attack'
fe_formula <- formula(model_supp_ent_blockFeatures, fixed.only = TRUE)
raw_predictors <- all.vars(fe_formula)[-1] 

# --- 3. Create numeric-only dataframe for correlation ---
model_data_numeric <- attack_clean_df #%>%
  
  # Select only the predictors that are actually in the final model
  #select(all_of(raw_predictors)) %>%
  
  # *** NEW DUMMY VARIABLE STEP ***
  # This will find any factor/character columns (like 'seg2_senti')
  # and create new 0/1 columns for each level (e.g., seg2_senti_POSITIVE)
  #dummy_cols(remove_selected_columns = TRUE, # Removes the original factor
  #           remove_first_dummy = FALSE)     # Keep all levels for the matrix

# --- 4. Calculate the correlation matrix ---
predictor_cor_matrix <- cor(attack_clean_df[, unique(final_features)], use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# --- 5. Filter for strong correlations ---
# (The rest of your code from before is perfect)
strong_correlations <- as.data.frame(predictor_cor_matrix) %>%
  rownames_to_column("Var1") %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Var1,
    names_to = "Var2",
    values_to = "Correlation"
  ) %>%
  filter(abs(Correlation) > 0.4 & abs(Correlation) < 1.0 & Var1 < Var2) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(Correlation)))

# --- 6. Print the final, clean table ---
print(strong_correlations)
```

```{r}
model_supp_ent_interact_per <- glmer(arg_nli_attack ~ (1|CorpusID) 
                        + prag_distance
                        + seg2_n_tokens
                        + seg2_impScore
                        + seg1_impScore
                        + seg1_n_tokens
                        + tfidf_cosine_similarity
                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity * seg2_perplexity
                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity * seg1_perplexity
                        + cosine_similarity
                        , data = attack_clean_df, family = binomial, control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))
```


```{r}
anova(model_att_con_baseline, model_att_con_blockFeatures, model_supp_ent_interact_per, test = "Chisq")
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_att_con_blockFeatures)
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_supp_ent_interact_per)
vif(model_att_con_blockFeatures)
vif(model_supp_ent_interact_per)

library(car)
car::Anova(model_supp_ent_interact_per)
```

```{r}
model_att_con_interact_prag_cos <- glmer(arg_nli_attack ~ (1|CorpusID) 
                        + prag_distance * cosine_similarity
                        + seg2_n_tokens
                        + seg2_impScore
                        + seg1_impScore
                        + seg1_n_tokens
                        + tfidf_cosine_similarity
                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity * seg2_perplexity
                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity * seg1_perplexity
                        #+ cosine_similarity
                        , data = attack_clean_df, family = binomial, control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))
```


```{r}
anova(model_supp_ent_baseline, model_att_con_blockFeatures, model_att_con_interact_prag_cos, test = "Chisq")
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_att_con_blockFeatures)
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_att_con_interact_prag_cos)
vif(model_att_con_blockFeatures)
vif(model_att_con_interact_prag_cos)

library(car)
car::Anova(model_att_con_interact_prag_cos)
```
```{r}
model_att_con_interact_tfidf_cos <- glmer(arg_nli_attack ~ (1|CorpusID) 
                        + prag_distance * cosine_similarity
                        + seg2_n_tokens
                        + seg2_impScore
                        + seg1_impScore
                        + seg1_n_tokens
                        + tfidf_cosine_similarity
                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity * seg2_perplexity
                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity * seg1_perplexity
                        + tfidf_cosine_similarity * cosine_similarity
                        #+ cosine_similarity
                        , data = attack_clean_df, family = binomial, control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))
```


```{r}
anova(model_att_con_baseline, model_att_con_interact_prag_cos, model_att_con_interact_tfidf_cos, test = "Chisq")
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_att_con_interact_prag_cos)
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_att_con_interact_tfidf_cos)
vif(model_att_con_interact_prag_cos)
vif(model_att_con_interact_tfidf_cos)

library(car)
car::Anova(model_att_con_interact_tfidf_cos)
```

```{r}
model_att_con_interact_tfidf_prag <- glmer(arg_nli_attack ~ (1|CorpusID) 
                        + prag_distance * cosine_similarity
                        + seg2_n_tokens
                        + seg2_impScore
                        + seg1_impScore
                        + seg1_n_tokens
                        + tfidf_cosine_similarity
                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity * seg2_perplexity
                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity * seg1_perplexity
                        + tfidf_cosine_similarity * cosine_similarity
                        + tfidf_cosine_similarity * prag_distance
                        , data = attack_clean_df, family = binomial, control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))
```


```{r}
anova(model_att_con_baseline, model_att_con_interact_prag_cos, model_att_con_interact_tfidf_prag, test = "Chisq")
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_att_con_interact_prag_cos)
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_att_con_interact_tfidf_prag)
vif(model_att_con_interact_prag_cos)
vif(model_att_con_interact_tfidf_prag)

library(car)
car::Anova(model_att_con_interact_tfidf_prag)
```
```{r}
model_att_con_interact_three_way <- glmer(arg_nli_attack ~ (1|CorpusID) 
                        + prag_distance * cosine_similarity
                        + seg2_n_tokens
                        + seg2_impScore
                        + seg1_impScore
                        + seg1_n_tokens
                        + tfidf_cosine_similarity
                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity * seg2_perplexity
                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity * seg1_perplexity
                        + tfidf_cosine_similarity * cosine_similarity * prag_distance
                        #+ tfidf_cosine_similarity 
                        , data = attack_clean_df, family = binomial, control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))
```


```{r}
anova(model_att_con_baseline, model_att_con_interact_tfidf_prag, model_att_con_interact_three_way, test = "Chisq")
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_att_con_interact_tfidf_prag)
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_att_con_interact_three_way)
vif(model_att_con_interact_tfidf_prag)
vif(model_att_con_interact_three_way)

library(car)
car::Anova(model_att_con_interact_three_way)
```


```{r}
model_att_con_interact_three_way_prag <- glmer(arg_nli_attack ~ (1|CorpusID) 
                        + prag_distance * cosine_similarity * seg2_given_seg1_perplexity
                        + seg2_n_tokens
                        + seg2_impScore
                        + seg1_impScore
                        + seg1_n_tokens
                        + tfidf_cosine_similarity
                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity * seg2_perplexity
                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity * seg1_perplexity
                        + tfidf_cosine_similarity * cosine_similarity * prag_distance
                        , data = attack_clean_df, family = binomial, control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))
```

```{r}
anova(model_att_con_baseline, model_att_con_interact_three_way, model_att_con_interact_three_way_prag, test = "Chisq")
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_att_con_interact_three_way)
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_att_con_interact_three_way_prag)
vif(model_att_con_interact_three_way)
vif(model_att_con_interact_three_way_prag)

library(car)
car::Anova(model_att_con_interact_three_way_prag)
```
```{r}
model_att_con_interact_imp <- glmer(arg_nli_attack ~ (1|CorpusID) 
                        + prag_distance * cosine_similarity
                        + seg2_n_tokens
                        + seg2_impScore * prag_distance
                        + seg1_impScore * prag_distance
                        + seg1_n_tokens
                        + tfidf_cosine_similarity
                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity * seg2_perplexity
                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity * seg1_perplexity
                        + tfidf_cosine_similarity * cosine_similarity * prag_distance
                        #+ tfidf_cosine_similarity 
                        , data = attack_clean_df, family = binomial, control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))
```

```{r}
anova(model_att_con_baseline, model_att_con_interact_three_way, model_att_con_interact_imp, test = "Chisq")
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_att_con_interact_three_way)
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_att_con_interact_imp)
vif(model_att_con_interact_three_way)
vif(model_att_con_interact_imp)

library(car)
car::Anova(model_att_con_interact_imp)
```

```{r}
model_att_con_interact_three_way_remove_marginal <- glmer(arg_nli_attack ~ (1|CorpusID) 
                        + prag_distance * cosine_similarity
                        + seg2_n_tokens
                        + seg2_impScore
                        #+ seg1_impScore * prag_distance
                        + seg1_n_tokens
                        + tfidf_cosine_similarity
                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity * seg2_perplexity
                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity * seg1_perplexity
                        + tfidf_cosine_similarity * cosine_similarity * prag_distance
                        #+ tfidf_cosine_similarity 
                        , data = attack_clean_df, family = binomial, control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))
```

```{r}
anova(model_att_con_baseline, model_att_con_interact_three_way, model_att_con_interact_three_way_remove_marginal, test = "Chisq")
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_att_con_interact_three_way)
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_att_con_interact_three_way_remove_marginal)
vif(model_att_con_interact_three_way)
vif(model_att_con_interact_three_way_remove_marginal)

library(car)
car::Anova(model_att_con_interact_three_way_remove_marginal)
```

```{r}
Anova(model_att_con_interact_three_way_remove_marginal)
```
```{r}
library(broom.mixed)
library(dplyr)

effects_table <- tidy(model_att_con_interact_three_way_remove_marginal, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  arrange(p.value) %>%
  mutate(significance = case_when(
    p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
    p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
    p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
    TRUE ~ ""
  ))

effects_table
```


```{r}
library(lme4)
library(performance)
library(broom.mixed)
library(purrr)

# Define your dropped blocks from earlier
blocks_dropped <- list(
  lexical_markers = c("seg1_has_deduction", "seg2_has_deduction",
                      "seg1_has_refutation", "seg2_has_refutation", 
                      "seg1_has_condition", "seg2_has_condition",
                      "seg1_has_explanation", "seg2_has_explanation", 
                      "seg1_has_adverb_of_emphasis", "seg2_has_adverb_of_emphasis",
                      "seg1_has_justification", "seg2_has_justification", 
                      "seg1_has_modal_verbs", "seg2_has_modal_verbs"),
  #lexical_sim = c("tfidf_cosine_similarity"),
  #semantic = c("cosine_similarity"),
  structure = c("seg1_sentence_length_mean", "seg2_sentence_length_mean"),
  #                     "seg1_n_tokens", "seg2_n_tokens"),
  syntactic = c("seg1_dependency_distance_mean", "seg2_dependency_distance_mean",
                "seg1_prop_adjacent_dependency_relation_mean", "seg2_prop_adjacent_dependency_relation_mean",
                "seg1_avg_subclauses", "seg2_avg_subclauses",
                "seg1_avg_tree_depth", "seg2_avg_tree_depth"),
  #perplexity = c("seg1_perplexity", "seg2_perplexity", #"seg1_given_seg2_perplexity", 
  #               "seg2_given_seg1_perplexity"),
  #indirectness = c("seg1_impScore", "seg2_impScore", "prag_distance"),
  sentiment = c("seg1_senti", "seg2_senti")
)

# Loop through them and test one-at-a-time addition
block_refit_results <- map_df(names(blocks_dropped), function(b) {
  predictors <- paste(blocks_dropped[[b]], collapse = " + ")
  m_block <- update(model_att_con_interact_three_way_remove_marginal, paste(". ~ . +", predictors))
  comp <- anova(model_att_con_interact_three_way_remove_marginal, m_block, test = "Chisq")
  tibble(
    block = b,
    delta_AIC = AIC(model_att_con_interact_three_way_remove_marginal) - AIC(m_block),
    LRT_p = comp$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2],
    R2_marg_gain = performance::r2_nakagawa(m_block)$R2_marginal -
                   performance::r2_nakagawa(model_att_con_interact_three_way_remove_marginal)$R2_marginal
  )
})

block_refit_results
```

```{r}
#summary(update(model_interact_three_way_refined, . ~ . + seg1_avg_tree_depth + seg2_avg_tree_depth))
car::Anova(update(model_att_con_interact_three_way_remove_marginal, . ~ . + seg1_has_deduction 
                                                + seg2_has_deduction
                                                + seg1_has_refutation
                                                + seg2_has_refutation 
                                                + seg1_has_condition
                                                + seg2_has_condition
                                                + seg1_has_explanation + seg2_has_explanation
                                                + seg1_has_adverb_of_emphasis + seg2_has_adverb_of_emphasis + seg1_has_justification + seg2_has_justification 
                                                + seg1_has_modal_verbs + seg2_has_modal_verbs), type="III")
```

```{r}
car::Anova(update(model_att_con_interact_three_way_remove_marginal, . ~ . + seg1_senti
                                                         + seg2_senti), type="III")
```

```{r}
car::Anova(update(model_att_con_interact_three_way_remove_marginal, . ~ . + seg1_dependency_distance_mean + seg2_dependency_distance_mean
                 + seg1_prop_adjacent_dependency_relation_mean + seg2_prop_adjacent_dependency_relation_mean
                 + seg1_avg_subclauses + seg2_avg_subclauses
                 + seg1_avg_tree_depth + seg2_avg_tree_depth), type="III")
```

```{r}
car::Anova(update(model_att_con_interact_three_way_remove_marginal , . ~ . + seg1_sentence_length_mean + seg2_sentence_length_mean), type="III")
```

```{r}
model_final_expanded <- glmer(arg_nli_attack ~ (1|CorpusID) 
                        + prag_distance * cosine_similarity
                        + seg2_n_tokens
                        + seg2_impScore
                        #+ seg1_impScore * prag_distance
                        + seg1_n_tokens
                        + tfidf_cosine_similarity
                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity * seg2_perplexity
                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity * seg1_perplexity
                        + tfidf_cosine_similarity * cosine_similarity * prag_distance
                        + seg1_has_deduction
                        + seg2_has_refutation
                        + seg1_has_condition
                        + seg2_has_condition
                        + seg2_has_explanation
                        + seg1_has_adverb_of_emphasis
                        + seg2_has_adverb_of_emphasis
                        + seg1_has_justification
                        + seg2_has_justification
                        + seg1_has_modal_verbs
                        + seg2_has_modal_verbs
                        + seg2_dependency_distance_mean
                        + seg1_prop_adjacent_dependency_relation_mean
                        + seg1_avg_subclauses
                        + seg1_avg_tree_depth
                        + seg1_senti
                        , data = attack_clean_df, family = binomial, control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))

```

```{r}
library(car)
anova(model_att_con_interact_three_way_remove_marginal, model_final_expanded, text="Chisq")
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_att_con_interact_three_way_remove_marginal)
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_final_expanded)
car::Anova(model_final_expanded, type="III")
performance::check_collinearity(model_final_expanded)
```

```{r}
model_final_expanded_remove_marginal <- glmer(arg_nli_attack ~ (1|CorpusID) 
                        + prag_distance * cosine_similarity
                        + seg2_n_tokens
                        + seg2_impScore
                        #+ seg1_impScore * prag_distance
                        + seg1_n_tokens
                        + tfidf_cosine_similarity
                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity * seg2_perplexity
                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity * seg1_perplexity
                        + tfidf_cosine_similarity * cosine_similarity * prag_distance
                        + seg1_has_deduction
                        + seg2_has_refutation
                        + seg1_has_condition
                        + seg2_has_condition
                        + seg2_has_explanation
                        + seg1_has_adverb_of_emphasis
                        + seg2_has_adverb_of_emphasis
                        + seg1_has_justification
                        + seg2_has_justification
                        #+ seg1_has_modal_verbs
                        + seg2_has_modal_verbs
                        + seg2_dependency_distance_mean
                        + seg1_prop_adjacent_dependency_relation_mean * seg1_avg_tree_depth
                        #+ seg1_avg_subclauses
                        + seg1_senti
                        , data = attack_clean_df, family = binomial, control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))
```

```{r}
library(car)
anova(model_att_con_interact_three_way_remove_marginal, model_final_expanded, model_final_expanded_remove_marginal, text="Chisq")
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_final_expanded)
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_final_expanded_remove_marginal)
car::Anova(model_final_expanded_remove_marginal, type="III")
performance::check_collinearity(model_final_expanded_remove_marginal)
```

```{r}
model_final_expanded_remove_marginal_refined <- glmer(arg_nli_attack ~ (1|CorpusID) 
                        + prag_distance #* cosine_similarity
                        + seg2_n_tokens
                        + seg2_impScore
                        #+ seg1_impScore * prag_distance
                        + seg1_n_tokens
                        + tfidf_cosine_similarity
                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity * seg2_perplexity
                        + seg2_given_seg1_perplexity * seg1_perplexity
                        + tfidf_cosine_similarity * cosine_similarity 
                        #+ cos* prag_distance
                        #+ seg1_has_deduction
                        + seg2_has_refutation
                        + seg1_has_condition
                        + seg2_has_condition
                        + seg2_has_explanation
                        + seg1_has_adverb_of_emphasis
                        + seg2_has_adverb_of_emphasis
                        + seg1_has_justification
                        + seg2_has_justification
                        #+ seg1_has_modal_verbs
                        + seg2_has_modal_verbs
                        + seg2_dependency_distance_mean
                        #+ seg1_prop_adjacent_dependency_relation_mean * seg1_avg_tree_depth
                        #+ seg1_avg_subclauses
                        + seg1_senti
                        , data = attack_clean_df, family = binomial, control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))
```

```{r}
library(car)
anova(model_att_con_interact_three_way_remove_marginal, model_final_expanded, model_final_expanded_remove_marginal, model_final_expanded_remove_marginal_refined, text="Chisq")
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_final_expanded)
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_final_expanded_remove_marginal)
performance::r2_nakagawa(model_final_expanded_remove_marginal_refined)
car::Anova(model_final_expanded_remove_marginal_refined, type="III")
performance::check_collinearity(model_final_expanded_remove_marginal_refined)
```

```{r}
# --- This is your MAIN results table ---
# It's simple, clean, and you already have it.
library(car)
model_significance <- car::Anova(model_final_expanded_remove_marginal_refined, type = "III")
print(model_significance)

# --- This table goes in your APPENDIX ---
library(broom.mixed)
appendix_table <- tidy(model_final_expanded_remove_marginal_refined,
                       effects = "fixed",
                       exponentiate = TRUE,
                       conf.int = TRUE)

print(appendix_table)

# --- This is the ONE-LINE solution ---
library(sjPlot)

# This command creates a beautiful, publication-ready
# HTML table right in your RStudio viewer.
tab_model(
  model_final_expanded_remove_marginal_refined, 
  dv.labels = "Predicting Entailment (is_attackt = 1)",
  p.style = "scientific"
)
```

```{r}
# --- Load necessary libraries ---
library(lme4)         # For glmer
library(broom.mixed)  # For tidy()
library(car)          # For Anova()
library(dplyr)        # For data manipulation (joining and cleaning)
library(tibble)       # For rownames_to_column()

# --- 1. Get Estimates, ORs, and CIs from tidy() ---
# We need to run tidy() twice:
# First, to get the raw log-odds (b) and Standard Error (SE)
model_estimates <- tidy(model_final_expanded_remove_marginal_refined,
                        effects = "fixed",
                        conf.int = FALSE) %>%
  select(term, b = estimate, std.error)

# Second, to get the exponentiated Odds Ratios (OR) and 95% CIs
model_ors <- tidy(model_final_expanded_remove_marginal_refined,
                  effects = "fixed",
                  exponentiate = TRUE,
                  conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  select(term, OR = estimate, conf.low, conf.high)

# --- 2. Get Significance (Chisq and p-value) from Anova() ---
# This is the more robust test for predictor significance
model_significance <- car::Anova(model_final_expanded_remove_marginal_refined, type = "III")

# Convert the Anova output to a clean data frame
significance_df <- as.data.frame(model_significance) %>%
  rownames_to_column("term") %>%
  select(term, Chisq, `p.value` = `Pr(>Chisq)`)

# --- 3. Join All Pieces Together ---
publication_table <- model_estimates %>%
  # Join with the Odds Ratios
  left_join(model_ors, by = "term") %>%
  # Join with the significance stats
  left_join(significance_df, by = "term") %>%
  
  # --- 4. Clean and Format for the Paper ---
  
  # Add a "Pretty Name" column (YOU MUST CUSTOMIZE THIS)
  mutate(Predictor = case_when(
    term == "(Intercept)" ~ "Intercept",
    term == "cosine_similarity" ~ "Semantic Similarity (Cosine)",
    term == "tfidf_cosine_similarity" ~ "Lexical Similarity (TF-IDF)",
    term == "seg2_given_seg1_perplexity" ~ "Conditional Perplexity (S2|S1)",
    term == "prag_distance" ~ "Pragmatic Distance",
    term == "seg2_impScore" ~ "Indirectness (Seg 2)",
    term == "seg1_perplexity" ~ "Perplexity (Seg 1)",
    term == "seg2_perplexity" ~ "Perplexity (Seg 2)",
    term == "seg2_n_tokens" ~ "Length (Seg 2 Tokens)",
    term == "seg1_senti" ~ "Sentiment (Seg 1)",
    term == "seg2_senti" ~ "Sentiment (Seg 2)",
    term == "seg2_has_deduction" ~ "Marker: Deduction (Seg2)",
    term == "seg1_has_refutation" ~ "Marker: Refutation (Seg 1)",
    term == "seg2_has_refutation" ~ "Marker: Refutation (Seg 2)",
    term == "seg1_has_justification" ~ "Marker: Justification (Seg 1)",
    term == "seg2_has_justification" ~ "Marker: Justification (Seg 2)",
    term == "seg2_has_adverb_of_emphasis " ~ "Marker: Adv of Emphasis (Seg 1)",
    term == "seg1_has_condition" ~ "Marker: Condition (Seg 1)",
    term == "seg1_has_modal_verbs" ~ "Marker: Modal (Seg 1)",
    term == "seg2_has_modal_verbs" ~ "Marker: Modal (Seg 2)",
    term == "seg2_dependency_distance_mean" ~ "Dependency Distance Mean (Seg 2)",
    term == "seg1_impScore" ~ "Indirectness (Seg 2)",
    # Interactions (indented for readability)
    term == "cosine_similarity:tfidf_cosine_similarity" ~ "  Sem. x Lex. Similarity",
    term == "cosine_similarity:seg2_given_seg1_perplexity" ~ "  Sem. Sim. x Cond. Perplexity",
    term == "prag_distance:seg2_impScore" ~ "  Prag. Dist. x Indirectness (S2)",
    term == "seg2_given_seg1_perplexity:seg1_perplexity" ~ "  Cond. Perp. x Perp. (S1)",
    term == "seg2_given_seg1_perplexity:seg2_perplexity" ~ "  Cond. Perp. x Perp. (S2)",
    term == "tfidf_cosine_similarity:seg2_given_seg1_perplexity" ~ "  Lex. Sim. x Cond. Perplexity",
    term == "cosine_similarity:tfidf_cosine_similarity:seg2_given_seg1_perplexity" ~ "  Sem. x Lex. x Cond. Perplexity",
    term == "seg2_avg_subclauses:seg2_dependency_distance_mean" ~ "Avg subclauses (S2) x Dependency distance Mean (S2)",
    term == "seg2_avg_subclauses:seg2_avg_tree_depth" ~ "Avg subclauses (S2) x Avg tree depth (S2)",
    term == "seg1_avg_tree_depth:seg1_dependency_distance_mean" ~ "Avg tree depth (S1) x Dependency distance mean (S1)",
    term == "seg2_dependency_distance_mean:seg2_avg_tree_depth" ~ "Dependency Distance Mean (S2) x Cond. Perplexity",
    TRUE ~ term # Fallback for any I missed
  )) %>%
  
  # Create the single 95% CI column
  mutate(`95% CI` = paste0("[", round(conf.low, 3), ", ", round(conf.high, 3), "]")) %>%
  
  # Select and rename the final columns to be publication-ready
  select(
    `Predictor` = Predictor,
    `$b$` = b,
    `SE` = std.error,
    `Odds Ratio` = OR,
    `95% CI`,
    `$\\chi^2$` = Chisq,
    `p-value` = p.value
  )

# --- 5. View Your Final Table ---
# You can now print this or use a package like 'knitr' or 'gt' to make it beautiful
print(publication_table, n = 30) # Print all rows
```
